cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So Jane quit her job on March 20, 2020. Now, 176 days have passed since then, and we need to find out what the date will be tomorrow in MM/DD/YYYY format. First, I need to calculate the date 176 days after March 20, 2020, and then add one more day to get tomorrow's date. Let me start by breaking down the days month by month. Starting with March 20, 2020. March has 31 days, so from March 20 to March 31 is 11 days (31 - 20 = 11, but since we include the starting day, maybe it's 11 days? Wait, no—if we start on March 20, the next day is March 21, so from March 20 to March 21 is 1 day. So from March 20 to March 31 is 11 days (31 - 20 = 11). But since we are starting on March 20, the first day is March 20, and the next day is March 21. So adding days after March 20 would be days starting from March 21. Hmm, maybe I need to think of it as the next day after March 20 is March 21, so the total days in March after March 20 is 11 days (from March 21 to March 31). So, 11 days in March. Then April has 30 days, May has 31, June 30, July 31, August 31, September 30, October 31, etc. Let me start adding these up. Starting from March 20, 2020: March 20 to March 31: 11 days remaining in March. So 11 days. Then April has 30 days. So 11 + 30 = 41 days. May has 31 days: 41 + 31 = 72 days. June has 30 days: 72 + 30 = 102 days. July has 31 days: 102 + 31 = 133 days. August has 31 days: 133 + 31 = 164 days. September has 30 days: 164 + 30 = 194 days. But we need 176 days. So after August, we have 164 days. So we need 176 - 164 = 12 more days into September. So the 176th day after March 20, 2020 is September 12, 2020. Wait, because August 31 is the end of August, so adding 1 day would be September 1. So adding 12 days would be September 12. Therefore, 176 days after March 20, 2020 is September 12, 2020. So tomorrow's date would be September 13, 2020. But let me double-check my math here. Wait, let me recount: March 20, 2020 is day 0. Then day 1 is March 21. So to get 176 days after March 20, we need to count 176 days starting from March 21. Let me try that again. March: March 20 is the starting date. So March has 31 days. So days in March after March 20: 31 - 20 = 11 days. So March contributes 11 days. Then April has 30, May 31, June 30, July 31, August 31, September... So adding up: March: 11 April: 30 (total 41) May: 31 (total 72) June: 30 (total 102) July: 31 (total 133) August: 31 (total 164) September: 176 - 164 = 12 days. So September 12, 2020. So the 176th day after March 20, 2020
